role: Historian and journalist; Lecturer, Clemson University
bio: Arash Azizi is an Iranian historian and journalist, currently a lecturer at Clemson University. He is the author of *The Shadow Commander: Soleimani, the US, and Iran's Global Ambitions* (2020), a biography of [Qasem Soleimani](/people/qasem-soleimani) that examines the Quds Force commander's operational career and the strategic logic of Iran's regional expansion. He is also the author of *What Iranians Want: Women, Life, Freedom* (2023), an account of the 2022 [Mahsa Amini protests](/events/mahsa-amini-protests-2022) and the generational shift inside Iran. Azizi is a regular commentator on Iranian politics, the Woman Life Freedom movement, and Iranian diaspora politics, contributing to publications including The Atlantic, Foreign Affairs, and the New York Times.
editorial_notes: Author of The Shadow Commander (Soleimani biography, 2020) and What Iranians Want (2023). Lecturer at Clemson. Previously based in New York.
